front: Novel: Septimus Harding challenged by John Bold over income from Hiram's Hospital; smear campaign by The Jupiter
Novel: Low Church Bishop Proudie and his domineering wife; chaplain Obadiah Slope delivers sermon against chanting
Novel: Eleanor Bold wooed by three men; slaps Slope; marries Mr. Arabin (appointed dean)
Novel: Augustus Melmotte partners with Hamilton Fisker in fake railroad from Salt Lake City to Veracruz; kills himself with prussic acid
back: The Way We Live Now (Anthony Trollope)

front: Novel: Dr. Pessimist Anticant parodies Carlyle; Mr. Popular Sentiment parodies Dickens
Novel: La Signora Neroni — crippled, seductive, flirts while lying on couch; father returned from 12 years collecting butterflies in Italy
Novel: Harding plays imaginary violoncello when stressed
Novel: Felix Carbury pursues Marie Melmotte; Lady Carbury begs editors to publish; inspired by Fanny Trollope's Domestic Manners
back: The Way We Live Now (Anthony Trollope)

front: Novel: Lizzie Greystock pursues Lord Fawn after Sir Florian's death; Mr. Camperdown tries to retrieve family heirloom diamonds
Author: introduced freestanding mailboxes to England; worked at General Post Office 30+ years; feuded with Rowland Hill
Author: mother Frances (Fanny) Trollope wrote Domestic Manners of the Americans
Novel: party at Ullathorne Court; Mr. Quiverful (14 children in Puddingdale) becomes new warden
Novel: Rev. Josiah Crawley blamed for stealing a check worth 20 pounds
back: The Last Chronicle of Barset (Anthony Trollope)

front: Novel: Phineas acquitted of murdering Mr. Bonteen after Emilius found to possess duplicate key
Author: wrote 47 novels; resigned Post Office to stand as Liberal candidate for Beverley
Novel: lawyer Squercum vs. firm Slow and Bideawhile; Emperor of China dinner; Evening Pulpit newspaper
back: The Way We Live Now (Anthony Trollope)
